publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-09-17 18:18:15 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Manuscripts, photographs, slides, printed matter, clippings, scrapbooks, broadsides, tape recordings, transcripts, historical data, correspndence, notes, records, costumes, mailings, abd materials.
did.abstract: Collection consists primarily of manuscript material for Bradley's book on the history of the Cornell Alumni Association, 1972, as well as numerous other items pertaining to Cornell history.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection consists primarily of manuscript material for Bradley's book on the history of the Cornell Alumni Association, 1972; Cornell photographs, glass lantern slides, printed matter, clippings, scrapbooks, broadsides, and other Cornelliana; tape recordings and transcripts of former members of the Alumni Office staff, including Foster Coffin, Emmet Murphy, Ray Ashbery, Max Schmitt, Olive N. Snyder, Herman Krauss, Frances Egan, Walter K. Nield, Ho Ballou, Howard Stevenson, and Willard Kiggins; historical data concerning Cornell organizations including Quill and Dagger, Alpha Delta Phi, Cornell Alumni Fund, Cornell University Council, and Cornell Clubs of New York, New England, and Chicago; track team programs, correspondence, and travel information; personal correspondence; Her Honor Forever Maintain, by Bradley, 1973, and associated notes, manuscripts, and sources; "How to Conduct Class Reunions," a 1961 speech by Bradley; Class of 1926 reunion and fundraising materials, class mailings, and reunion costumes; records pertaining to other Cornell classes; and a cassette recording of Hunt Bradley's memorial service, 1982. Also, 1 bottle (empty) of Ballantine Burton Ale brewed for Hunt Bradley, 1946, and an evaluation of the contents, 1985.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Hunt Bradley papers, #41-2-1550. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Fund raisers (Persons).
Educational fund raising.
Class reunions.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Office of Alumni Affairs

did.unittitle: All-around Jumping and Weight Throwing contests
Cornell - Class of '26 Class Dinners at NYCC
"How to Conduct Class Reunions," a paper presented to the American Alumni Council National Conference
Reunion letters - Brewer and Munschauer Promotional Personal
American Alumni Council Dist. II Conference
Bradley book: Her Honor Forever Maintain
History - Title on cover, Title Page, Frontispiece, Introduction, Dedication, Chapter Heads
Cornell Alumni Corporation - Notes on annual meetings, Board of Directors meetings, etc.
Elements of Style, Strunk, and notes
Bradley's notes on the Associate Alumni Meetings Minutes
History - Pictures - photo copies, master set #1
Transcript of tape recording: Foster Coffin '12
Transcript of tape recording: Emmet Murphy '22
Transcript of tape recording: Ray Ashbery '25
Transcript of tape recording: Max Schmitt '24
Transcript of tape recording: Olive N. Snyder '22, Walter K. Nield '27, Herman Knauss '27 (no transcript, questions only: Willard Kiggins '21, Francis Eagan '26, Howard Stevenson '19)
Scrapbook of newspaper clippings (field and track)
Cornell Club of New England Minutes
Memorabilia: Class of '26 plaque, jacket, cap, buttons; also, brass plated plate from Public Affairs Staff,
Olive N. Snyder '22 and Frances Egan '26a
Ho Ballou tape - old days at Cornell (not for history)
